## On 2 separate occasions- Alabama

On 2 separate occasions- Alabama - Seattle - purchased Diet Dr Pepper - 3 12 pk cans - 3 16 oz bottles. Cans out dated with a nasty taste - bottles Flat. Customer service took all information- assured there would be coupons sent as means of compensation. Both were in 2024 - July - Nov. to date NOTHING- yet I got “rate” the associate- listed my complaint of no receipt- still nothing. What a rip off -

raw_text:
[The 7 Best Pens of 2026 | Reviews by Wirecutter - ny times]: The Pilot G2, though highly popular, didn’t compare well against other pens. It skipped, feathered, and blobbed more than our picks, and it also took a notably long time to dry.

The Sharpie Pen is technically a marker with a very fine tip, so it feels different from nearly any other pen, and that made it divisive for our testers.

Schneider pens, including the Slider Rave XB and Slider Basic, are smoother and faster-drying than a typical cheap office pen. But these German-made pens aren’t as widely available as our picks, and we found the Basic’s thin grip less comfortable to hold than those of other pens we tested. [...] Michael Hession/NYT Wirecutter

#### Best for...

.

It’s comfortable to hold. Thanks to its relatively light weight and soft grip section, the Jetstream Slim Multi-Color doesn’t wear out your hand during extended writing periods.

It looks great, too. We appreciated the subtle, stylish, light colors the pen comes in (including white, pink, mint green, and lavender).

### Flaws but not dealbreakers

It has one notable flaw. Our main complaint about the design is that the color of each ink is imprinted on the body of the pen, not on the knock. So if you reload the pen with different colors or gel inks, you’ll have to remember which knock belongs to which color.

### Is this your company?

Claim your profile to access Trustpilot’s free business tools and connect with customers.

### The Trustpilot Experience

#### We're open to all [...] I was the biggest fan of Dr Pepper. Sadly, last week, I purchased a can of the cherry rush limited edition, drank half of it, emptied the rest of the can from the liquid to throw it away (it wasn’t as tasty as the normal Dr Pepper), and as I was about to throw it, I sensed it was still quite heavy for an empty can. I shook it and it was obvious something was in the can, but not liquid. I turned it upside down to try and have a look at what is inside, and I saw a tissue-like thingy in it! Needless to say how disgusted I was, and I had diarrhea for 2 days. The can is still with me and still not wide open as I was trying to reach out to the consumer care dpt, which after a week from the email correspondence and providing them with images and details, they simply sent a letter to say that [...] images and details, they simply sent a letter to say that most probably this is mould that happened in poor storage conditions which they have nothing to do with as they have no control over the storage conditions buyers (shops) store their purchased goods in. I will be sharing a video soon on social media, opening the can and finding out what is really in there. Thank you Dr Pepper, you made me realize how poor and useless your consumer care dpt actually is, and I propose you remove the word ‘care’
